column1: # Constraint Table for SU2_conformal Model in PTArcade-Preferred Region

column2: Beam dump constraints on sub-GeV dark gauge bosons
column13: Production and decay of dark gauge bosons via kinetic mixing
column15: dark gauge boson decay width,dark gauge boson production rate
column18: NOT_APPLICABLE - Model has no kinetic mixing (epsilon=0)
column19: Model has no SM portal couplings (epsilon=0, lambda_HPhi=0) as per assumptions; Without kinetic mixing or Higgs portal, dark gauge bosons cannot be produced at beam dumps; If portal couplings are added, beam dump constraints would become relevant for m_W ~ 0.3-0.6 GeV

column2: Collider mono-X constraints on sub-GeV states
column13: Invisible decay of dark sector particles
column18: NOT_APPLICABLE - No portal couplings in model
column19: Model assumes no portal interactions (lambda_HPhi=0, epsilon=0); Without production mechanism via SM, collider constraints do not apply; If Higgs portal is added, mono-Higgs or VBF+MET searches could constrain

column13: Electron scattering cross section via kinetic mixing
column19: Requires kinetic mixing epsilon > 0 for electron coupling; Model has epsilon=0 by assumption

column17: annihilation cross section for W+ W- -> scalars
column18: APPROXIMATELY_TESTABLE - Literature discusses non-thermal production mechanism
column19: Literature (2109.11558) finds viable non-thermal vector DM for v ~ 100-10000 GeV; At v ~ 0.6-1.3 GeV, masses are too low for standard freeze-out but non-thermal production from SM bath via scalar portal may still work; Custodial SO(3) symmetry (from SSB pattern) may stabilize vector DM

column15: W+ W- -> W+ W- scattering cross section,W W -> rho rho cross section
column18: REQUIRES_NEW_BACKEND - Need to compute dark sector scattering
column19: Vector DM self-interaction via gauge coupling g_D; Cross section ~ g_D^4 / m_W^2 ~ 10^-2 to 1 cm^2/g for PTA region; Galactic constraints: sigma/m < 0.1 cm^2/g; Cluster constraints: sigma/m < 1 cm^2/g; May be relevant for m_W ~ 0.3-0.6 GeV

column2: BBN constraints on light scalar decays
column19: m_rho ~ 36-86 MeV, m_G ~ 18-43 MeV in PTA-preferred region; BBN constraints apply for lifetimes > 1 second or electromagnetic/strong decays; Without portal couplings to SM, scalars are stable on cosmological timescales; If gravitationally coupled only, decays are negligible; But model may need to check if scalars contribute to N_eff or dark radiation

column13: Effective number of neutrino species N_eff
column15: dark sector temperature evolution,decoupling temperature,relic abundance at T_CMB
column17: dark sector thermal history,decoupling temperature from SM
column19: Model assumes xi=1 (thermal equilibrium with SM); But if dark sector decouples before QCD, xi may differ; FOPT at T_* << v ~ 0.6-1.3 GeV, so after QCD epoch; Dark sector particles with m < T_CMB ~ 10^-4 GeV would contribute to N_eff; In PTA region, all dark particles have m > 18 MeV, so non-relativistic at CMB; But if T_reh > T_CMB, dark sector may be reheated and contribute

m_W ~ 0.3-0.6 GeV corresponds to warm DM regime; Free-streaming length ~ 100 kpc to 1 Mpc for these masses; Lyman-alpha forest constraints: m_W > 1-10 keV for cold DM (well satisfied); Warm DM constraints: m_W > 1-5 keV (well satisfied for m_W ~ 300-600 MeV); No strong structure formation constraints for these masses

SN1987A constraints require production via SM interactions; Model has no portal couplings (epsilon=0, lambda_HPhi=0); Dark sector particles cannot be produced in SN core; If produced via kinetic mixing, constraints would be severe for m < 100 MeV

column2: Stellar cooling constraints (horizontal branch, red giants)
column19: Stellar constraints require production via electromagnetic or weak interactions; Model has no portal couplings to SM gauge bosons or fermions; Without portal, dark sector particles cannot be produced in stars

column18: REQUIRES_RECAST - Need portal coupling for production
column19: LEP, BaBar searched for light particles in e+e- collisions; Without portal coupling to photons or Z, no production mechanism; With kinetic mixing epsilon, LEP constrains epsilon > 10^-2 for m < 1 GeV
DIRECTLY_TESTABLE - Can compute from model parameters
column20: g_D ~ 0.91-0.98 is perturbative (g_D^2/4pi ~ 0.07); beta_lambda = 3*g_D^4/(64*pi^2) ~ 0.003-0.004 > 0, valid for CW; Model assumptions include perturbativity: g_D < 4*pi ~ 12.56; PTA region well within perturbative regime

column2: Strong supercooling and high-T expansion validity
column14: thermal potential comparison with 3D EFT,nucleation temperature
column18: PTA-preferred region has alpha >> 0.5 (up to 10^16); High-temperature expansion assumes T ~ v, but T_* << v in supercooling; Literature (2312.12413) notes challenges in EFT description for strong supercooling; 3D EFT (dimensional reduction) may be more accurate but not implemented in backend

column17: DIRECTLY_TESTABLE - Template selected for large alpha
column18: PTA region has alpha >> 0.5 (up to 10^16); dbf (dissipative bulk flow) template from 2511.15687 validated for large alpha; higgsless template only valid for alpha <= 0.5, inappropriate here; bf template also valid but dbf preferred; Backend uses fixed v_w=1.0, cs=1/sqrt(3) - may need validation

column16: g_star(T) for dark sector with xi=1
column18: Model assumes xi=1 (T_D = T_SM); With strong supercooling, T_* can be much lower than v; From fopt_benchmarks: T_* ~ 3-5 MeV for v ~ 0.6-1.3 GeV; g_star(T_* ~ MeV) ~ 10-20, need to verify in backend calculation

Backend uses Gaussian approximation for false vacuum fraction and percolation condition; 200 out of 400 scan points failed due to eternal inflation; PTA-preferred region points (from benchmark scan) all marked as viable; Need to verify percolation condition holds in PTA 1-sigma region
